description:
Why do so many drivers dread the Chesapeake Bay Bridge?

For some, it is the height, the narrow lanes, the wind, and the long stretch of water with no shoulder and nowhere to stop. For others, it is still one of Maryland’s proudest engineering achievements, a landmark that changed the state forever. In this episode of Engineering the Impossible, we take a deep look at how the Chesapeake Bay Bridge was built, why it feels so different to drive, how traffic and maintenance have reshaped its story over the decades, and why Maryland is now facing one of the biggest infrastructure decisions in its history.

From the ferry era and the original 1952 span to the added 1973 crossing, barrier failures, deck rehabilitation, lane-control changes, and the new urgency that followed the collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge, this is the story of a bridge that solved one historic problem and slowly became another.

If you have crossed the Chesapeake Bay Bridge, or if you have a story about it, share it in the comments below.
